title: This presumption in favour of grammatical interpretation was stated by a nineteenth-century Lord Chancellor, Lord Selborne, in the words 'there is always some presumption in favour of the more simple and literal interpretation of the words of the statute'.

evidence.clean_text: This presumption in favour of grammatical interpretation was stated by a nineteenth-century Lord Chancellor, Lord Selborne, in the words 'there is always some presumption in favour of the more simple and literal interpretation of the words of the statute' [Caledonian Rly Co v North British Rly Co (1881) 6 App Cas 114 at 121]
